evals.name: java-dml-backfill-placeholders
evals.prompt: I need a Java Spring Data MongoDB change to update all user documents and set 'active' to true. I don't have the order or author yet.
evals.expected_output: A Java DML class using placeholders for order and author, correctly injecting MongoTemplate.

evals.name: kotlin-ddl-create-collection-idempotent
evals.prompt: Create a Kotlin Spring Data MongoDB DDL change to create a collection 'audits'.
evals.expected_output: A Kotlin DDL class with transactional=false and an idempotency check.
expectations: Checks if 'audits' collection exists before creating or uses ensureIndex safely

evals.name: java-dml-springdata-semantics
evals.prompt: Create a DML change in Java to delete inactive users using Spring Data MongoDB.
evals.expected_output: A Java DML class where data operations are expressed with MongoTemplate rather than sync-driver session APIs.

evals.name: mixed-ddl-dml-refusal
evals.prompt: Create one Java Flamingock change that creates a 'logs' collection and inserts a START document into it using Spring Data MongoDB.
evals.expected_output: A refusal to mix DDL and DML in one Spring Data MongoDB change, with a split into two changes.
expectations: Refuses a single mixed DDL/DML class
Explains that DDL must be non-transactional and DML stays in Spring Data semantics
Proposes separate DDL and DML changes

evals.name: redirect-to-targetsystem-when-config-missing
evals.prompt: Write a Spring Data MongoDB Flamingock change for my users collection, but I still haven't configured the MongoDB Spring Data TargetSystem yet.
evals.expected_output: A redirection to the MongoDB Spring Data TargetSystem skill instead of generating the change immediately.
expectations: Recognizes that the TargetSystem setup is missing
Does not generate the migration class immediately
